import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def task_func(df: pd.DataFrame, column_name: str) -> (str, plt.Axes):
    if df.empty or (column_name not in df.columns and column_name != ''):
        fig, ax = plt.subplots()
        fig.suptitle("Distribution of values in " + column_name + " (No Data)", fontsize=16)
        return ("The DataFrame is empty or the specified column has no data.", ax)

    values = df[column_name].dropna().unique()
    n = len(values)

    if n == 1:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots()
        fig.suptitle("Distribution of values in " + column_name, fontsize=16)
        ax.bar([0], [1])
        return ("The distribution of values is uniform.", ax)

    frequencies = [len(df[df[column_name] == value]) / len(df) for value in values]
    max_freq = max(frequencies)

    if abs(max_freq - 1/n) < 0.05:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots()
        fig.suptitle("Distribution of values in " + column_name, fontsize=16)
        ax.bar(values, frequencies, alpha=0.7)
        ax.set_xlabel('Values')
        ax.set_ylabel('Frequency')
        return ("The distribution of values is uniform.", ax)
    else:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots()
        fig.suptitle("Distribution of values in " + column_name, fontsize=16)
        ax.bar(values, frequencies, alpha=0.7)
        ax.set_xlabel('Values')
        ax.set_ylabel('Frequency')
        return ("The distribution of values is not uniform.", ax)
